reviews.review_description: As someone who regularly plays escape room games, this was amazing. There were a wide variety of different puzzles that were actually challenging as opposed to the super simple repetitive ones that a lot of other games do. The story was really neat and creepy. The graphics and detail, the lantern showing what the house looked like before, the acting and the ability to walk around the memories as they play out is just absolutely amazing. I love this game so much that I bought it for my friend so that she could play it as well. This is now my favorite game. I also really liked the fact that you had other objectives like trying to collect all the hidden coins. The only downside to this game is that others are going to be crap now that I've played this. I've been spoiled.

This is a unique game. I have recently developed a love of puzzle games and this one did not disappoint. It does offer hints and there are ways they allow you to bypass challenges if you get frustrated and want to move on. Graphics are decent and I really enjoyed the pace of the story arc. While it's not a game I would play more than once, that one time was more than worth it. Definitely recommend you at least give it a try.

This is a great puzzle game. The graphics are great and the characters look cool in VR since they are real actors in a kind of ghostly volumetric video. The puzzles are interesting, but some of them are the hardest puzzles that I've seen in a VR game. Luckily the game provides hints, and there are a few hidden coins in the environment that each allows you to skip a puzzle, so you can still progress in the game by skipping a few of the hardest ones. If you like games like Red Matter 1 & 2, Ghost Town, and The Room VR, then you will probably love this game , especially if you like a challenge.

Ok im about 98% complete in this game, and i have to say i loved every minute of it! The puzzles are so unique, and challenging, and i say this after playing nearly all the escape room games. You really need to think! Some of the best puzzles were the hat, the mini stage, the jewells, the soldier pin game. But whats really amazing with this game is the whole layout and the picture frames that when the lantern shines evil on them they turn into a grotesque evil painting. So much artwork and thought went into designing this game. Feels like a masterpiece! Having the actors in there for real life made me feell like i was in a movie. Well done to the team!

This was one of my first games as a PC owner in the 90's, it came with multiple disks and it was so exciting! I never did finish it.. Now i get to do it all over in VR! And I don't even need a CD ROM! They did a great job on this one a true work of mystery art! Take my money!

reviews.review_title: Everyone should play this at least once
reviews.review_description:
I stepped into this game thinking it would be scarier, but honestly, it’s perfect without it. The puzzles, the story, beautiful, and the live action hologram bits? Brilliant! I love every little detail in this, and the demonic clue vibes work great, especially with the changed paintings, it was fun to see how each one changed. I ended up finishing the whole game in one night, that’s how much I was into it, great experience. 

Some bits about the puzzles, might just be me, but I couldn’t figure out the block puzzle, and got kinda stuck on the cockroaches cause I didn’t know you had to turn the knobs. Grateful for the hint system though.

Great graphics and sound effects, but the story line is not very interesting and the hints are too complicated to figure out out by yourself. A lot of walking in circles. I gave up. Not really worth the price.

I love this game.  The puzzles are just hard enough without being so hard that you lose patience.  I do have 1 issue.  Because of a glitch in the game I was able to get to the basement before I was supposed to.  I didn’t realize it at the time. I solved those puzzles earlier than I was supposed to.  Now I’m at the point of where I should be if I had solved the basement in the proper order but the game isn’t allowing me to proceed to what comes next. I’ve watched videos online of what happens after the basement.  As I said I have solved all the puzzles that should be solved before the basement.  But I think since I went out of order, the game is confused so I’m stuck.  Hoping someone can help.

reviews.review_title: The NOSTALGIA. Omg SO WELL DONE
reviews.review_description:
Listen, were there some small glitches? Sure, but they were all easily resolved and rarely occurred. But this SERIOUSLY brought me back to the 90’s place this on my cousins Mac desktop and my mom having to buy me a thesaurus to solve one of the puzzles. I sing this games praises ever chance I get.

So, when I saw this was available for the Oculus, I jumped on it and was NOT disappointed. Great graphics, great variety of puzzles and games (could not solve one puzzle, will replay soon so I can try again). It only took me about week, an hour or two a night, to complete. I am SO SO sad it’s over.

I will play anything you guys release, I’m such a huge huge fan!!!!
The doll nose won’t attach in the final chapter meaning you have to start the game over. Terrible design.
Items get stuck in your hand, the progression panel often doesn't work. Cannot complete the game due to bugs.

I played this when it first came out for PC, I still have the CD, and this is definitely the best way to play it! It was wonderful to explore everything in VR. It all just felt so much more engaging than the flat version. I quit playing the PC version after a few rooms, but I have now completed the game, in VR, 30ish years later. Hahaha

The puzzles are extremely hard, often there is no logic to them. The hints on the help board are very poor. They should say more about a method how to solve a puzzle. 
Some are so senseless you will end up buying the puzzle out which is poor value and ruins the game. The controllers are glitchy often dont do what they supposed to, and grabbing thing is difficult  because the light is in your hand. 
Its difficult to know what sequence the puzzles should be solved. Which is often important. 
Very frustrating game, but the concept is good.
Lantern lost at last level. Cant complete game. This should have been fixed as long as this game has been out.

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR experiences
reviews.review_description: The puzzles were excellent! Except the drinking game one which drove me nuts. The spaces and controls, especially for the puzzles, were so well implemented specifically for VR. The volumetric video capture for the characters was such a great idea and looked great. I'm surprised more studios don't implement the technique which helps take the load off the limits of the hardware. The 1930s aesthetics felt spot on along with the music. I can keep going but this is simply a must buy for narrative, puzzle, and VR fans in general.

I'm someone who has played the original game ( way back on my mother's iMac) and could never finish the game because I was so scared and couldn't understand some of the puzzles; I was young, so don't hate. With this new VR version, I'm blown away by the amazing gameplay, puzzles, story and nice little Easter eggs from the original game ( the song on the record in the music room and the outro song during the credits); just beautiful odes to the original. I love this game and am so impressed. I'm hopeful for a VR version of the 11th Hour 🤩
One of the best Puzzle games
Love this so much. Beautiful graphics, great voices. Some puzzles little bit too hard for me but gladly you can use hints. I wish there were more games like this more than shooters or fighter games.
Really fun game

I bought this game for Christmas so glad I didn't pay the whole lot as it was on sale . The planchette doesn't work , I've completed loads of puzzles three rooms ,  whenever I go to use the planchette it just comes up with map  , help , and  progression  , and when I click on it nothing happens they light up but that's all I've reinstalled it three times rebooted my headset and nothing  .
 I've solved  loads to puzzles and not with the planchette whenever I go on to it nothing happens cant you use the coins .
Very disappointing
I enjoyed it.  If u like puzzles it’s definitely a good game.

reviews.review_title: Feels like a Murder Mystery Movie!
reviews.review_description:
As the title suggests, you feel like you are part of an unfolding murder mystery movie. 

Great storytelling, great graphics (Quest 3 with HD optimizer) though could have richer blacks in places. Difficult but extremely satisfying puzzles (only needed help twice via the ouija board)


Top title. Vertigo are AAA.

The 7th guest been my first Vr game, i really loved spend my time exploring the mansion and solve puzzles.
Dont miss this jewel!
This was an exceptionally crafted gaming experience. The haunted mansion experience I was hoping for.  Thank you!

A great puzzle game with a story. Love the atmosphere of just walking around the creepy old mansion. Graphics are very detailed and the puzzles work amazingly in VR. A few puzzles were a bit obtuse but the hint system is very useful in giving hints or letting you bypass a puzzle so it’s not a huge problem. 
I finished this game and would definitely recommend to puzzle fans and anyone who enjoys the haunted mansion ride at Disney.
Great game. Puzzles are great and just enough hard. I realy enjoyed this game.
Puzzles drive me crazy, difficult to solve some of the challenges.  Most of the Puzzles or scenes where fun, train set was my favorite. I thought the puppet room was sick.

reviews.review_title: How Did They Make This Good Of A Game
reviews.review_description: It was such an awesome experience
Top

Having played and enjoyed the original game back in the day I was slightly trepidatious about revisiting a classic. I didn't need to be. It was as fun as I remembered plus some of the more annoying puzzles (I'm looking at you, tedious cellar maze) that really wouldn't work in VR have been replaced by more  interesting and engaging puzzles. Kudos to the game makers (lovely to see the Trilobyte logo in the credits), you've really bought this game alive in VR. Great work!

I am 35 percent through this game and I’ve run into a glitch (in the kitchen). I’ve looked at a You Tube walk through of this room so I know for certain it’s a glitch. I tried going out of the game and turning off my headset to no avail. Are there any other options I should try?
Great game.  Loved this game back in 90s.  In VR it's so okay incredible!  Excellent storyline, good puzzles and a lot to explore and interact with.  Highly recommend.

Immersive storyline mixed with some of the most creative puzzles found in a VR game.  The cinematic scenes are extremely well done and look great on the Quest. Unlike others, I didn't have have any issues accessing the hints which were definitely needed at times.  Overall, great experience.

We really enjoyed the mystery and murder story of the game. Loved that the puzzles stayed within the room, so you don't have to wonder through the house. The puzzles slowly got harder, but still enjoying. Hope more of these come out!

reviews.review_title: Good graphics, weak story and gameplay
reviews.review_description:
The graphics are truly excellent and atmospheric.
However, there’s barely any story, the actors are terrible - and completely unnecessary anyway.
The puzzles are nice enough but hardly challenging. I never once felt the need to use a hint. The controls are clunky - this has been solved far better in many other games.
The finale is a sluggish dice-rolling sequence without any depth whatsoever.
Overall, unfortunately, only mediocre. A pity, because the atmospheric setting could have offered so much more.

On a side note: the 18+ rating in Germany is rather strange - there’s nothing particularly violent or scary here.
I have several games like this!! This one by far has the most rooms and I ok me to longest to get through!! Loved having the hints for when I got stuck!!

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR adventures to date
reviews.review_description: Very good recreation of the cult classic PC adventure. Very good quality of chalenging puzzles, very interesting mystery. Not scary in a bad way (no jump scare etc.), but quite intense atmosphere. One gripe is to the help and map system (wooden board), which didn't work for me. For me it was much better than Ghost Town.

Motion sickness: 5/5.  Tons of options with snap turn and teleport.  It is not an action game and just walking, solving puzzles and watching people talk.

The game: About 25% of the puzzles I could not figure out.  Unlike other reviews the hint system worked fine for me although it did not always help me figure out what to do.  There are hidden coins that can auto solve puzzles.  It cannot be abused as there is a limit but it is greatly appreciated over being stuck.

Most of the rooms are somewhat uninspired.  The last battle seemed like a step up but I was touching all the controls and nothing was happening.   I used a coin instead.

The right score imo is 70%

overall a great game just like the original.  has a small bug with the ouija board.  if you're a board is always blank quit the game completely and reopen. that fixed it for me.  

the clues aren't very great, there are mostly very vague and then go straight to solving it for you. 

the texture is a really good, very good vr unlike the rest of the metaverse. takes a very dark turn towards the end so definitely not for children.
Great game but to many glitches.

Great game if you're a fan of the original. Looks great and plays great. Puzzles are definitely easier than the original game, but that is not a bad thing (needed a hint book before the internet!). Definitely give it a try.
unnerving atmosphere and great puzzles 👌

I loved this game but I'm going to give it 1 star.
From the start, the Spirit Board did not work for me. I could make it appear, but trying to use the pointer to move to get help hints, or to know how many stars you had collected was futile.
All I could see was the map.
Persevering, I made it through the game to the last room (no spoilers!) As with any room, if you leave without solving, it SHOULD reset for when you return to allow you to work it through. This doesn't really happen on the last room. The piece in the box returns there, but it is no longer possible to place it on the face. Those pieces no longer move so you basically cannot complete the game.
I have tried reaching out to the developers who provide nothing buy an automated auto reply saying they  are busy and might take a few days to respond; that was over 3 months ago. 
My only option is to reinstall the game (it is not one that saves to your headset) and go through the whole thing again, with no guarantee that I won't have the same problem again.
So great game in principle, but 1 star for crap customer service from the developer.

I really do wish there were more games like this available on Meta.   This game was intelligently and creatively developed.   Everything about the game is good, I have nothing negative to say.  I really hope these developers create more content like this.  Great work guys!!! 💯

The game itself was  great for about 7 chapters, but suddenly at 64% my save refuses to load and just dumps me back to my Meta desktop.  No error code, no explanation.  Meta checked out my Quest 2, found no issues, and referred me to Vertigo Games support site where I started a support ticket.  That was on June 8th.  I got one email saying they were busy but would get to me but I never heard back, and they have ignored follow up emails.  A few weeks ago I checked again but had to fill out another ticket with no response.  It was a good game until it wasn’t, and being ghosted buy tech support doesn’t give me the warm fuzzies.  Buyer beware.

reviews.review_title: Good value, long play time. Enjoyed it.
reviews.review_description: Seems some had technical problems but thankfully all ok for me. Found it challenging and did need to resort to a walkthrough to help me understand some puzzles. The help board worked ok (again for me though some had troubles with it). Having done Ghost Town just prior to this, I felt this was better value, much longer play time and good graphics. Storyline was weird but ok.

This game is stunning in visuals and very creative and fun puzzles, but the spirit board doesn't always work, so you can't get any hints or skips for puzzles.  You have to solve everything yourself to progress in the game.

reviews.review_title: Built for Comfort, Made for Mystery
reviews.review_description:
​I have to say, The 7th Guest VR is a game that just pulls you in. It's so atmospheric and fun, with a great sense of an old, creepy house without being full of jump scares. As someone who has to be careful with VR games, I've been so happy with the customisable options. There are a ton of choices for movement, which is a big relief for people who get motion sickness or migraines. The fact that they even included an option for a sitting or standing camera is a huge plus and makes the game much more accessible for anyone with a chronic illness or mobility difficulties.
​The puzzles are really fun, and I love that there's a nice mix of different types, so there's something to enjoy for everyone. There were a couple of times I got stuck, but it's great that they included hints as an option—it really helps to keep the game feeling relaxed and not frustrating.
​The acting clips are done so well, and the whole game is paced perfectly for me. I just kept wanting to explore every room and figure out what happened next.

Visual is nice  but, I think Video game testers should have worked a little longer on this one. Many technical bugs, not many instruction for the spirit board. Took me a bit to figure out how to work with it. Would have been nice to have levels of difficulty.
It was fun for me i never played 7th Guest before as I didn't had good enough multimedia PC those time. Now I enjoyed VR modern version. 5 stars. Good puzzle game.
I loved this game so much the puzzles were great hopefully you’ll bring another game out

reviews.review_title: Good game but geez, the controls
reviews.review_description:
The story and puzzles are pretty good. Looks really nice too. A few puzzles were a bit confusing but I only needed one solved for me (chess, ugh). The controls though were incredibly frustrating. Things get stuck in your hand, objects can be difficult to pick up or just glitch all over the place. Like the piano puzzle requires you press one key at a time. No big deal until the controls press the same key multiple times and you have to start all over. 
Buy it when it's on sale.

reviews.review_title: Super fun and easy on the stomach
reviews.review_description: I enjoyed this game so much and I didn't get as sick .... it was fun. The puzzles ate easy when they make sense ... some puzzles I had a hard time figuring out not because of the puzzle part of them but of the actual game mecganics... I felt some were super clear and then some were weird . Like the piano one ...

Update. When I re run the chapter my magic lantern was available.. Maybe a small glitch.  Finished it but needed to use the solve option for the final chapter. Why? Because my magic lantern disappeared when I entered the area and I couldn't go back. Had the lens to attach to the lamp but no lamp ! I intend to go again as it's such an enjoyable experience but I'm wondering did I forget something or did a bug disappear my lamp? 🤔😁. It reminds me of an old Vincent Price movie, House on Haunted Hill, where guests had to survive a night in a haunted mansion👀👻😱. Having just finished Ghost Town, I thought this will be for me, and it is!  I'm thinking that maybe the puzzles will be a bit more difficult than in Ghost Town, but we will see. I'm definitely recommending this one. This is exactly the type of game I like. Spooky atmosphere, puzzles and a story behind the game.
Very good game. One of my favorite puzzle solving games in VR. Highly recommend

Back in the 90s and today games like Myst were too hard. This game had good puzzle design without making you search for a guide every 5 seconds.

One of the most beautiful quest games in the market and I’m using a quest 2.

I hope we get more 90s FMV games that are easier than Myst .

reviews.review_title: After 30y in love again with 7th Guest!!
reviews.review_description: This game is great! Gfx, music and sounds are wonderful on Quest3. The riddles are not as hard as the originals back in the 90s but very entertaining. The actors did a good job and the 3D implemantation is extremly fascinating. 8-10h with nice atmosphere and gameplay. You should give it a try!

I loved the original. Beat it many times over and even its sequel (which was still fun but not quite as good). This really captured the spooky atmosphere while keeping it fresh enough for those of us who played the original. My only gripe is that the puzzles were hit or miss. Some were fun, while others a bit awkward and didnt land well. But it was the cool story and atmosphere that made this game a hit for me.

​As a new VR player, The 7th Guest VR has completely blown me away. 

The visual fidelity and atmosphere of Stauf Mansion are simply incredible and set a high bar for VR puzzle games. The polish is exceptional, especially considering the price point—it feels like a premium experience.

​The immersion is so strong that I barely noticed the outside world; I was so focused on the environment that I actually noticed the light coming through my headset’s nose gap for the very first time!

​While I’m still early in the game, this initial experience confirms it's a must-buy for any VR enthusiast. I can't wait to see what other secrets this eerie, visually stunning mansion holds.

This game seems unusually long for a VR title (in a good way). It's still shorter than most non VR games, but if you take your time looking around and you're not a whiz at point and click adventure game puzzles, it will probably take more than one sitting to get through and be very fun along the way. 

Completely Non Plot or Game Related Spoiler Ahead. Beware.  Be sure to look at all of the paintings with your flashlight before completing that room's puzzles. There aren't any clues in them but they're interesting as hell

This was one of the first games I played on my VR headset and I loved it. The beginning was a great build up and then entering the mansion with all of the rooms to unlock. On top of great puzzles and finding coins, the actors that built the story, were in a 3D scene that gave me goosebumps. It spoiled me for other games as this was such an amazing experience. I didn't want it to end.

I just got done with ghost town vr and this one is just as good very well done I will say tho that I thought ghost town had a slightly better story but this game is a 9 out of 10 a must play for a good story and puzzles
Recommend if you enjoy puzzle games. Very immersive.

This game was absolutely incredible with the best puzzles and the graphics were fantastic. I really enjoyed the storyline and the level of detail that is obvious the moment you start the game. I really like how each room contains everything you need to complete that rooms puzzles. I hate when you have to spend tons of time trying to figure out what room a single item is located in. The actual puzzles were each unique and actually super fun. I did have to use my spirit board a few times for hints and the hints were enough to lead me in figuring out what I needed to do. I also enjoyed the amount of rooms to explore and I was sad when I finished this game. I really hope that there will be a sequel soon🙏🙏 This game is worth every penny! ❤️❤️❤️

When I first bought this game, I tried it for a few minutes then decided to try something else.  THEN later I gave the game another chance.  One of my best VR decisions.  This game was a delight to play.  The graphics are amazing and the player movement was fluid and easy.  The puzzles were a blast to solve.  There were a few that I had to roll a gold coin, but overall very fun.  Would highly recommend this game!!

I cannot express enough how much I loved this game! The puzzles, graphics, and story are all fantastic and incredibly enjoyable. The puzzles strike a great balance; they are neither too easy nor too difficult. I especially enjoyed the lantern abilities—they added a lot of fun to the gameplay. 

However, I did encounter some issues in the nursery. It was challenging to place the dolls on the guillotine or the chairs, and that's something the developers should address. Despite this minor issue, it didn't detract from my overall enjoyment of the game.
Thoroughly enjoyed this game on every level. The game, puzzles and story are excellently woven together to provide a lasting challenge with a fair difficulty curve. Worth every penny.
Better than the ROOM, amazing game with great story.

I don't understand how this is not 5 stars. :

- The graphics are awesome.
- Atmosphere and story awesome.
- Puzzles awesome and not as random as with some other 'escape room' type of games.
- Game mechanics fluid and not as clunky as I've experienced with some other games.

The only thing I dislike is that I will finish the game at some point and cannot play it again for the first time, I hope the team that build this will make many new games!

reviews.review_title: Ideal puzzle game with amazing graphics
reviews.review_description:
I am an avid puzzle game player with a soft spot for amazing graphics and a compelling story line. This game has it all!
I also don't do well with horror games but rest assured that this game is not meant to scare you but does mean to create a creepy atmosphere that adds to the story line.
Puzzels were just challenging enough to make you think, but never too hard. Also loved the length of the game which really makes the game worth its money. Would recommend!

reviews.review_title: Perfect for those new to VR
reviews.review_description:
Loved this game back in the day. Everything has been remade for VR perfectly. 
It is also PERFECT if you are new to VR. I hadn’t used a headset since the first gen and got quest3 and instantly gave myself VR sickness. This game was great for getting my sea legs back and getting used to the way VR games work when they are well made. The puzzles are perfectly balanced and the atmosphere and story are great. Highly recommended

I have had nothing but fun playing this game! Every level is different, it keeps me busy, it makes me critically think, it’s just one of the best games I’ve played in a while. If you are looking for a game that almost feels like an escape room, this would 100% be for you

reviews.review_title: Beautiful to watch, quite tricky to play
reviews.review_description:
The graphics and atmosphere are top notch, the puzzles are ok, but there are some issues with controls and maybe some bugs; it's very cluncky when you have to do precise movements, also a puzzle actually freezed and I couldn't solve it (not even checking online to see if I was doing something wrong) and I accidentally skipped the second-last room by entering in the last one before.
So, overall it's an ok puzzle game with some minor frustrating issues.

I love puzzle games, and this game delivered!  Had such a good time that I shared it with my kids.    The only issues are that the instructions can be rather ambiguous, and the controls can be a bit wonky when trying to solve the more delicate puzzles.  Sometimes, I felt like a bull in a China shop.  Highly recommend!

I grew up playing this with my dad, and loved the puzzles and the mansion itself.    This game is honestly the only reason I bought the metaquest.  I couldn't wait to actually walk the halls, enter the rooms, and solve the puzzles I grew up with.  I was a little nervous that I had hyped it up in my head and that in the end I would be disappointed...but I wasn't at all!  It was way beyond what I had imagined!  Not only did all the nostalgia come flooding back, but a new awe came with it as well!  I loved the small changes to the house - though the maze in the basement would've been fun.  The puzzles were new with a couple having some fun ties to the originals.  I can't wait to have my dad over to show him the game/house in VR.  Hopefully there are plans to do the 11th hour in the future!!

I had never played this game on PC and managed to get it at a good discount for Meta. I found the story and puzzles sensational. It’s a game with an acceptable length. I recommend buying it on sale because it’s worth it.
I love this game. I played this first on my PC. It was great but now I'm in another world.  I love it!!!!. Now I need the 11th hour.

Played this when I was a kid. Even though I only found one puzzle that I remember from the original one, I really liked this version. There were some very minor issues with detailed controls but that didn’t really bother me overall :)

I remember playing the original PC version when I was younger. Was one of my favorites. Absolutely loving this version just as much. Definitely recommend!

Some puzzles I did notice were missing, like the foyer puzzle, the maze, the coffin puzzle, but all in all, loved that most rooms had more than one puzzle that worked with the other ones in the same room.
This game was so fun I didn't want to finish it! It kept me challenged and interested with all the rooms and puzzles. I'm ready for a sequel, lol!
